pattern: The apple total inside a square of half-width r is a cubic in r, so binary search the smallest r meeting the requirement and return its perimeter.
hints: By symmetry the apples inside the square of half-width r depend only on r; derive a closed form for that total.
Summing |i|+|j| over all cells with |i|,|j| <= r gives 2*r*(r+1)*(2r+1) apples.
Find the smallest r with 2*r*(r+1)*(2r+1) >= neededApples (binary search or solve), then the side length is 2r and the perimeter is 8r.

edge_inputs.description: exactly the radius-1 total of twelve
one above a perfect total forces the next radius

stress.description: random required apple counts across the full range
